explicit_text: My name is William Nyang'un, born in Nairobi, Kenya in 1990 as the youngest of five children. My mother moved to the United States when I was just six months old, and I stayed in Kenya with my extended family until I joined my siblings in the U.S. in 2012. I have pursued my education here, starting at Minneapolis Community and Technical College and now studying Computer Science at the University of Minnesota. Despite the challenges, including losing my mother to cancer in 2013, my family has been a strong support system for me.

explicit_text: I was born and raised in Kenya, specifically in Nairobi, until I moved to the United States in 2012. Growing up in Kenya, I was cared for by my extended family after my mother moved to the U.S. when I was six months old. Kenya is where my roots are, and I plan to give back by starting an IT company there to help less fortunate children with their education. My cultural upbringing in Kenya, with the community involved in raising children, shaped who I am today.

explicit_text: The United States has always been my dream destination, and I finally moved here in 2012 to join my siblings and pursue my education. My mother moved here first when I was just six months old, and later my siblings followed before I arrived. I first lived in Virginia with my family, where we celebrated our first united Christmas in 2012, then I moved to Minnesota for college. The U.S. has given me opportunities to study and grow, even though I faced challenges like adjusting to the cold winters.

explicit_text: I was born in Nairobi, Kenya, on December 9th, 1990, and spent my early years there surrounded by my extended family. Nairobi is where I grew up after my mother left for the United States when I was six months old. It was in Nairobi that my family and community raised me, celebrating my birthdays and guiding me through life. Even though I moved to the U.S. later, Nairobi remains my hometown and the place I hope to return to one day to contribute through my work.

explicit_text: When I first moved to the United States in 2012, I lived in Virginia with my mother and siblings. That first Christmas together in Virginia was very special because it was the first time all of us siblings were united under one roof. We celebrated with a mix of African and American food, embracing both cultures. Virginia was my initial home in the U.S. before I moved to Minnesota to continue my education.

explicit_text: After living in Virginia, I moved to Minnesota to attend college starting in the spring of 2013. Minnesota was a big adjustment, especially because of the cold winters, which my brothers now tease me about since I survived them. I studied at Minneapolis Community and Technical College before transferring to the University of Minnesota. Minnesota has been an important place for my education and personal growth.

This college was my first step in pursuing higher education in the United States. It helped me adjust to academic life here and prepared me for transferring to a university. My experience there was foundational for my studies in Computer Science.
